is_vowel(Char) ->
    case Char of
        'a' -> true;
        'e' -> true;
        'i' -> true;
        'o' -> true;
        'u' -> true;
        _ -> false
    end.

% Helper to calculate frequency and first appearance index for all vowels
% Returns a list of {Vowel, Frequency, FirstIndex} tuples
calculate_vowel_stats(s) ->
    % Map: Vowel -> {Count, FirstIndex}
    Vowels = ['a', 'e', 'i', 'o', 'u'],
    initial_map = #{},

    % Calculate stats by iterating over the string s (list of characters)
    {stats, _} = lists:foldl(
        fun(Acc, Char) ->
            if is_vowel(Char) ->
                case maps:find(Char, Acc#stats) of
                    {Count, FirstIndex} when is_tuple(FirstIndex) ->
                        % Vowel seen before
                        Acc#stats#{Char => {Count + 1, FirstIndex}}
                    _ ->
                        % First time seeing this vowel
                        Acc#stats#{Char => {1, {Index}}}
                end
            else
                Acc
            end
        end,
        initial_map,
        s,
        Index
    ),

    % Convert map to list of {Vowel, Frequency, FirstIndex}
    lists:map(fun({Vowel, {Count, FirstIndex}}) -> {Vowel, Count, FirstIndex} end, maps:to_list(stats)).

% Helper to sort the vowel stats
% Sorting criteria:
% 1. Frequency (descending)
% 2. First Index (ascending)
sort_vowels(Stats) ->
    lists:sort(fun({V1, F1, I1}, {V2, F2, I2}) ->
        if F1 =:= F2 ->
            % Frequencies are equal, sort by FirstIndex ascending
            lists:compare(I1, I2)
        else
            % Sort by Frequency descending (F1 > F2 means V1 comes first -> negative comparison)
            if F1 > F2 -> -1;
            F1 < F2 -> 1;
            true -> 0
            end
        end
    end, Stats).

% Helper to build the ordered sequence of all vowels
build_vowel_sequence(SortedStats) ->
    lists:foldl(
        fun(Acc, {Vowel, Frequency, _}) ->
            lists:duplicate(Frequency, Vowel) ++ Acc
        end,
        [],
        SortedStats
    ).

solve(s) ->
    % 1. Get stats
    Stats = calculate_vowel_stats(s),

    % 2. Sort stats
    SortedStats = sort_vowels(Stats),

    % 3. Build the ordered sequence of all vowels
    VowelSequence = build_vowel_sequence(SortedStats),

    % 4. Reconstruct the string
    % We iterate over s, if it's a vowel, we replace it with the next vowel from VowelSequence
    {Result, _} = lists:foldl(
        fun(Acc, Char) ->
            if is_vowel(Char) ->
                % Consume the next vowel from the sequence
                case VowelSequence of
                    [] -> {Acc, []}; % Should not happen if logic is correct
                    [NextVowel | Rest] ->
                        {NextVowel ++ Acc, Rest}
                end
            else
                % Consonant, keep it
                {Char ++ Acc, VowelSequence}
            end
        end,
        {[], VowelSequence}, % Initial accumulator: {ResultList (reversed), RemainingSequence}
        s
    ),

    % The result list is built in reverse order
    lists:reverse(Result).
```

%% Helper functions for Erlang string/list operations might be defined here, but we will keep it contained in solve/1 if possible using standard library calls.

solve(s) ->
    % 1. Identify vowels and their positions
    Vowels = ["a", "e", "i", "o", "u"],
    Length = length(s),
    VowelSlots = lists:foldl(fun(Slots, {Index, Char}) ->
        if lists:member(Char, Vowels) ->
            lists:append(Slots, [{Index, Char}])
        false ->
            Slots
    end, [], {0, s}),

    % 2. Calculate Frequencies
    Counts = lists:foldl(fun(Map, Char) ->
        case maps:get(Char, Map) of
            none -> maps:put(Char, 1, Map);
            Count -> maps:put(Char, Count + 1, Map)
        end
    end, maps:new(), s),

    % 3. Determine First Appearance Index
    FirstIndices = lists:foldl(fun(Map, {Index, Char}) ->
        case maps:get(Char, Map) of
            none -> maps:put(Char, Index, Map);
            _ -> Map
        end
    end, maps:new(), VowelSlots),

    % 4. Gather unique vowels and prepare sorting keys
    UniqueVowels = maps:keys(Counts),

    % Create sorting tuples: {-Frequency, FirstIndex, VowelChar}
    SortKeys = lists:map(fun(Vowel) ->
        Freq = maps:get(Vowel, Counts),
        Index = maps:get(Vowel, FirstIndices),
        {-Freq, Index, Vowel}
    end, UniqueVowels),

    % 5. Sort the vowels
    % Standard Erlang list sorting is ascending. Since we negated frequency, 
    % sorting ascendingly achieves descending frequency order.
    SortedKeys = lists:sort(SortKeys),

    % Extract the sorted vowel characters
    SortedVowelList = lists:map(fun(_Freq, _Index, Vowel) -> Vowel end, SortedKeys),

    % 6. Generate the final sequence of vowels (V')
    % We need to expand the sorted characters based on their original counts.
    % Since SortedKeys already reflects the required order, we iterate over it 
    % and append the vowel character Count times.
    SortedVowelSequence = lists:foldl(fun(Accumulator, {_Freq, _Index, Vowel}) ->
        Count = maps:get(Vowel, Counts),
        lists:append(Accumulator, lists:duplicate(Vowel, Count))
    end, [], SortedKeys),

    % 7. Reconstruct the String
    % We need to iterate over the original string s and replace vowels using SortedVowelSequence.
    
    % Initialize sequence pointer
    {Result, VowelSeq} = {[], SortedVowelSequence},
    
    % Iterate through the original string s and the vowel sequence simultaneously
    FinalList = lists:foldl(fun({Acc, Seq}, Char) ->
        if lists:member(Char, Vowels) ->
            % It's a vowel, replace it with the next character from the sorted sequence
            case Seq of

        end

    % The fold builds the list in reverse order, so reverse it back

%% Helper functions for Erlang string/list operations might be defined here, but we will keep it contained in solve/1 if possible using standard library calls.

solve(s) ->
    % 1. Identify vowels and their positions
    Vowels = ["a", "e", "i", "o", "u"],
    Length = length(s),
    VowelSlots = lists:foldl(fun(Slots, {Index, Char}) ->
        if lists:member(Char, Vowels) ->
            lists:append(Slots, [{Index, Char}])
        false ->
            Slots
    end, [], {0, s}),

    % 2. Calculate Frequencies
    Counts = lists:foldl(fun(Map, Char) ->
        case maps:get(Char, Map) of
            none -> maps:put(Char, 1, Map);
            Count -> maps:put(Char, Count + 1, Map)
        end
    end, maps:new(), s),

    % 3. Determine First Appearance Index
    FirstIndices = lists:foldl(fun(Map, {Index, Char}) ->
        case maps:get(Char, Map) of
            none -> maps:put(Char, Index, Map);
            _ -> Map
        end
    end, maps:new(), VowelSlots),

    % 4. Gather unique vowels and prepare sorting keys
    UniqueVowels = maps:keys(Counts),

    % Create sorting tuples: {-Frequency, FirstIndex, VowelChar}
    SortKeys = lists:map(fun(Vowel) ->
        Freq = maps:get(Vowel, Counts),
        Index = maps:get(Vowel, FirstIndices),
        {-Freq, Index, Vowel}
    end, UniqueVowels),

    % 5. Sort the vowels
    % Standard Erlang list sorting is ascending. Since we negated frequency, 
    % sorting ascendingly achieves descending frequency order.
    SortedKeys = lists:sort(SortKeys),

    % Extract the sorted vowel characters
    SortedVowelList = lists:map(fun(_Freq, _Index, Vowel) -> Vowel end, SortedKeys),

    % 6. Generate the final sequence of vowels (V')
    % We need to expand the sorted characters based on their original counts.
    % Since SortedKeys already reflects the required order, we iterate over it 
    % and append the vowel character Count times.
    SortedVowelSequence = lists:foldl(fun(Accumulator, {_Freq, _Index, Vowel}) ->
        Count = maps:get(Vowel, Counts),
        lists:append(Accumulator, lists:duplicate(Vowel, Count))
    end, [], SortedKeys),

    % 7. Reconstruct the String
    % We need to iterate over the original string s and replace vowels using SortedVowelSequence.
    
    % Initialize sequence pointer
    {Result, VowelSeq} = {[], SortedVowelSequence},
    
    % Iterate through the original string s and the vowel sequence simultaneously
    FinalList = lists:foldl(fun({Acc, Seq}, Char) ->
        if lists:member(Char, Vowels) ->
            % It's a vowel, replace it with the next character from the sorted sequence
            case Seq of
                [] -> {lists:append(Acc, [Char]), Seq}; % Should not happen if counts match
                [NextVowel | RestSeq] -> {lists:append(Acc, [NextVowel]), RestSeq}
            end
        else
            % It's a consonant, keep it
            {lists:append(Acc, [Char]), Seq}
        end
    end, {[], SortedVowelSequence}, s),
    
    % The fold builds the list in reverse order, so reverse it back
    lists:reverse(FinalList).

    reconstruct_string_rec(S, VowelMultiset, 0, []).

reconstruct_string_rec([], _, _, Result) ->
    lists:reverse(Result);

reconstruct_string_rec([C|Rest], VowelMultiset, VowelIdx, Result) when is_vowel(C) ->
    % Replace with the next vowel from the multiset
    case lists:nth(VowelIdx + 1, VowelMultiset) of
        Vowel ->
            reconstruct_string_rec(Rest, VowelMultiset, VowelIdx + 1, [Vowel|Result]);
        _ ->
            % Should not happen if VowelMultiset is constructed correctly
            reconstruct_string_rec(Rest, VowelMultiset, VowelIdx, [C|Result])
    end;
reconstruct_string_rec([C|Rest], VowelMultiset, VowelIdx, Result) ->
    % Consonant, keep it
    reconstruct_string_rec(Rest, VowelMultiset, VowelIdx, [C|Result]).
